pattern: The last non-empty string consists of the characters whose total frequency equals the maximum frequency, preserved in their last-occurrence order within s.
hints: Each round of operations removes one occurrence of every distinct character present; a character survives until the final round only if its frequency equals the maximum frequency across all characters.
The characters remaining just before the last operation are exactly those with the maximum frequency in s.
Their relative order in the result equals their last occurrence order in s, since each round removes the first occurrence and the last survivor keeps the order of its final appearance.

edge_inputs.description: all distinct characters, whole string is the answer
multiple characters share the max frequency
only one character has the highest count
max-frequency chars appear at various positions

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghij') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
